key_insight: Increasing the barrel filling ratio from 43% to 53% produced a denser Cr3C2–NiCr detonation-sprayed coating with better wear resistance.
engineering_relevance: Optimizing detonation spraying fill ratio can improve protective coating performance for components exposed to severe friction and wear.
method: Detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coatings were deposited at 43% and 53% barrel filling ratios under identical conditions.
Microstructure and phases were analyzed to compare density, porosity, carbide distribution, and carbide transformation.
Dry sliding ball-on-disc wear tests were run at 10 and 15 N and 5 and 10 cm/s.
evidence_signal: XRD showed dominant Cr3C2 and NiCr with minor Cr7C3 formation, and the 53% coating achieved a minimum wear rate of 1.23 × 10^-4 mm3/(m·N).
watch_item: The higher-filling-ratio coating improved wear resistance but also showed a higher coefficient of friction, so application tradeoffs should be checked.
paper_title: Effect of Barrel Filling Ratio on the Microstructure, Phase Composition and Tribological Performance of Detonation-Sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr Coatings
